The Marigold Society
Seed LibraryThe AlmanacMembershipShopVisit
Greenhouse
Onceupon

in a quiet Garden

A society of growers, gatherers, and gardeners, keeping the seeds our grandmothers loved.

✦

The Almanac No. 9 — Summer is now available. Read the issue

✦
Strafford, Vermont — Est. 2021

A Society built on the premise that certain things are worth keeping.

We began as a seed swap in a church basement in Burlington — Estelle with her eight hundred packets of heirloom seeds and Oliver with a van and a folder of handwritten notes. What grew from that afternoon became something we hadn't entirely planned for: a community of people who care, with some intensity, about old varieties and good paper and the particular quality of attention that gardening demands.

The Marigold Society is now a seed library, a pressed-flower atelier, a quarterly journal, and a small farm in Strafford where visitors are always welcome during the growing season. We remain, as we began, a society: a gathering of people who find meaning in the same things.

Read our storyVisit the farm

The Seed Garden

Strafford, Vermont — June

400+

heirloom varieties in the library

The Seed Library

From the current season's selection

Browse all 400+ varieties
Gentle
Flower

Tagetes erecta 'Lady Margaret'

Lady Margaret Marigold

Deep amber-saffron blooms of startling complexity, each petal edged in a darker copper that deepens through the season.

$6.5075 days
Gentle
Vegetable

Phaseolus coccineus 'Vermont Scarlet'

Vermont Scarlet Runner

Vivid vermillion flowers cascade down sturdy vines all summer, followed by dramatic purple-black pods and richly flavored beans.

$5.0070 days
Patient
Flower

Alcea rosea 'Nigra'

Antwerp Black Hollyhock

Flowers of such deep burgundy they appear nearly black in evening light, rising on stately 8-foot spires above wide, velvety leaves.

$7.002nd year
Patient
Grain

Zea mays 'Four Corners Blue'

Four Corners Blue Corn

Deep indigo-black kernels of extraordinary nutty sweetness, cultivated for over eight centuries in the canyon lands of the American Southwest.

$8.00100 days

Join as a Cultivator to receive seeds quarterly.

Selected by Estelle and Oliver each season.

Explore membership
The Almanac — A Quarterly Journal

Written for those who read slowly.

Eighty pages of essays, growing notes, material culture, and the kind of field observation that doesn't fit anywhere else. Printed on uncoated stock and mailed to your door, quarterly.

Read the current issue
Craft

On the Quiet Art of Keeping

The Almanac No. 7 — Winter — by Estelle Marchand

There is a particular kind of silence that descends over a garden in late October, when the first hard frost has silvered everything and the long labour of growing is finished. It is in this silence that I do my most important work.

Continue reading
History

The Memory of Flowers

A short history of pressing, and why it has never gone out of fashion

6 min readThomas Alderidge
Growing

The Garden at the Edge of Things

What a Vermont winter teaches you about patience, failure, and next year

7 min readMarguerite Voss

Issue No. 9 — Summer 2025 — now available

The Membership

People who know what they're looking for.

“The Almanac is the only thing I read the day it arrives. I read it with coffee and I read it slowly.”

Philippa Rowe

Dorset, Vermont — since Spring 2021

P

“We grow things here that we don't know the names of. We just call them 'the ones from the Marigold Society' and people always want to know more.”

James & Tomoko Holt

Middlebury, Vermont — since Autumn 2021

J

“There are things in the seed library I cannot find anywhere else. That matters.”

Rosalind Church

Montpelier, Vermont — since Winter 2022

R

“My grandfather would have understood what the Marigold Society is doing. He saved everything. He knew what was worth keeping.”

Henri Beaumont

St. Johnsbury, Vermont — since Spring 2022

H

“I come home from a shift and I go straight to the garden before I do anything else. It restores something.”

Dr. Sarah Osei

Burlington, Vermont — since Autumn 2022

D

“The edible flowers from these seeds are on the menu. People ask where they come from and I tell them: from people who care.”

Beatrice & Luc Fontaine

Shelburne, Vermont — since Spring 2023

B

“The Almanac is the only thing I read the day it arrives. I read it with coffee and I read it slowly.”

Philippa Rowe

Dorset, Vermont — since Spring 2021

The Quarterly Box

Join the Society.

Three subscription tiers. Seeds from the library, the printed Almanac, and access to the Greenhouse — our members' forum where the conversations that started in the seed library continue year-round.

Explore membershipGive as a gift

Joined by 1,400 members across Vermont and beyond

The Marigold Society

A Vermont seed library, pressed-flower atelier, and quarterly journal for those who believe the old ways are worth preserving.

Find Us

18 Old Mill Road
Strafford, Vermont 05072

Write to Us

hello@marigoldsociety.com
